title: Redundant casts should be avoided
description: Casting expressions are utilized to convert one data type to another, such as transforming an integer into a string. This is especially crucial in strongly typed languages like C, C++, C#, Java, Python, and others. However, there are instances where casting expressions are not needed.
recommendation: Replace with the recommended alternative approach.

noncompliant:
int value = 42;
int result = (int)value; // Noncompliant - redundant cast

string text = "hello";
string upper = (string)text.ToUpper(); // Noncompliant - ToUpper() already returns string
compliant:
int value = 42;
int result = value; // OK - no cast needed

string text = "hello";
string upper = text.ToUpper(); // OK - no redundant cast

object obj = "test";
string str = (string)obj; // OK - necessary cast from object to string

logic.detection_conditions: Identifies explicit type casts that are redundant due to implicit type conversion
logic.triggering_conditions: Pattern matches explicit casts like '(int)intVariable' or '(string)stringMethod()' where the cast is unnecessary
exclusion_conditions: Necessary casts between different numeric types (long to int)
Casts for object or var types (boxing/unboxing)
never_trigger_on: Necessary numeric conversions (long to int, double to float)
logic.reasoning: Detects redundant explicit type casts where implicit conversion would work. The pattern identifies casts to primitive types and string method calls that don't require explicit casting. Excludes necessary casts between different numeric types and object scenarios where explicit casting is required.
